response:
We reproduced the failing security-audit gate locally (the GH Actions run was blocked by Rackspace SAML on the gh token) and found the blocker was a pinned vulnerable dependency: aiohttp==3.13.3. pip-audit reported 10 GHSA advisories with the first fixed in 3.13.4.

Remedy:
- Bumped aiohttp to the fixed version in both manifests: functions/requirements.txt and functions-webhook/requirements.txt -> aiohttp==3.13.4.
- Re-ran pip-audit for both requirements files (no known vulnerabilities found) and ran the test suite (./.venv-3.13/bin/pytest functions/tests -q — 454 passed).

(Separately we also removed Safety from the security pipeline and left pip-audit as the blocking scanner.)
context_injected:
{"ns": "azure-msp", "task": "compliance", "facts": ["automation_policies_topic_trigger depends_on azr-fleet-platform-events", "APIM authentication debugging pattern uses APIM policy XML", "AMA v3 implements reconciliation loop"], "recent": ["2026-04-06: In fleet-platform-app on 2026-04-06, reproduced a failing security-audit CI dependency gate locally when GitHub Actions inspection was blocked by Rackspace SAML on the gh token. Root cause was aiohttp pinned to 3.13.3 in both functions/requirements.txt and functions-webhook/requirements.txt; pip-audit reported 10 GHSA advisories with first fixed version 3.13.4. Updated both manifests to aiohttp==3.13.4, reran pip-audit for functions and webhook requirements with no known vulnerabilities found, and ran ./.venv-3.13/bin/pytest functions/tests -q successfully (454 passed).
[specifics: repository: fleet-platform-app; aiohttp: 3.13.3 pinned in functions/requirements.txt and functions-webhook/requirements.txt; pip-audit_advisories: 10 GHSA advisories reported; first_fixed_aiohttp_version: 3.13.4; aiohttp_updated_to: aiohttp==3.13.4 in functions/requirements.txt and functions-webhook/requirements.txt; pip-audit_after_update: no known vulnerabilities found; pytest_command: ./.venv-3.13/bin/pytest functions/tests -q; pytest_passed_count: 454; ci_inspection_blocked_by: Rackspace SAML on gh token]", "2026-04-06: On 2026-04-06 in fleet-platform-app, removed Safety from the security pipeline after confirming pip-audit remains the correct blocking dependency scanner and bandit/ruff do not cover package CVEs. Updated .github/workflows/security-audit.yml to install only pip-audit, removed both informational Safety scan steps, and stopped uploading safety-results.json. Removed safety==3.7.0 from functions/requirements-dev.txt. Verification: no remaining safety references in the active workflow or dev requirements.
[specifics: project: fleet-platform-app; workflow_file: .github/workflows/security-audit.yml; removed_package: safety==3.7.0; removed_from: functions/requirements-dev.txt; stopped_uploading: safety-results.json; blocking_scanner: pip-audit; informational_scans_removed: Safety scan steps removed from security-audit.yml; coverage_note: bandit and ruff do not cover package CVEs; verification: no remaining safety references in active workflow or dev requirements]", "2026-03-27: For fleet-platform-app PR #74 on 2026-03-27, the failing GitHub 'Pre-Commit (Gate)' job was not a broad hook miss; CI ran file-scoped pre-commit on changed files and failed only on Bandit B110 in functions/shared/deployment_conflicts.py due to two bare except/pass blocks. Fixed by removing the unnecessary try around response.text extraction and narrowing response.json exception handling to (AttributeError, TypeError, ValueError). Verified with `PRE_COMMIT_HOME=/tmp/pre-commit-cache ./.venv-3.13/bin/pre-commit run --files functions/shared/deployment_conflicts.py --color=always`, then committed as e95db3c and pushed to dev.
[specifics: pr: 74; job: Pre-Commit (Gate); file: functions/shared/deployment_conflicts.py; issue: Bandit B110 (bare except/pass); exceptions_allowed: (AttributeError, TypeError, ValueError); pre_commit_command: PRE_COMMIT_HOME=/tmp/pre-commit-cache ./.venv-3.13/bin/pre-commit run --files functions/shared/deployment_conflicts.py --color=always; commit: e95db3c; pre_commit_scope: file-scoped on changed files; venv_path: ./.venv-3.13; branch: dev]", "2026-03-27: For azr-fleet-platform PR #70 on 2026-03-27, failing Test Suite jobs were caused by GitHub Actions service container startup failing before checkout, not product tests. Root cause from run 23655768486: Docker Hub rate limiting on postgres:15-alpine with error 'toomanyrequests: too many failed login attempts for username or IP address'. Fixed locally in commit 1277cce by replacing postgres:15-alpine with public.ecr.aws/docker/library/postgres:15-alpine in .github/workflows/test.yml and the analogous test jobs in deploy-fleet-dev.yml and deploy-fleet-prod.yml. Left unrelated local change in .github/workflows/security-audit.yml uncommitted.
[specifics: pr_number: 70; run_id: 23655768486; commit: 1277cce; error: toomanyrequests: too many failed login attempts for username or IP address; original_image: postgres:15-alpine; replacement_image: public.ecr.aws/docker/library/postgres:15-alpine; files_modified: .github/workflows/test.yml, .github/workflows/deploy-fleet-dev.yml, .github/workflows/deploy-fleet-prod.yml; uncommitted_change: .github/workflows/security-audit.yml; failure_stage: GitHub Actions service container startup failing before checkout; root_cause: Docker Hub rate limiting on postgres:15-alpine]", "2026-03-27: Implemented and validated two production regression fixes in fleet-platform-app on branch dev: (1) AMA DCR deployment now treats transient ARM conflicts like 'Data collection rule has been modified before operation completed' as retryable, waits for the canonical Rackspace DCR to materialize with the expected rax-config-version, and reuses it if present; tests in functions/tests/test_ama_create_dcr_conflicts.py. (2) Bulk callback path no longer sends source_run_id in governance registration payload, preventing downstream FK failures against bulk_automation_runs, and Janus callback handling now treats target-state-equals-current-state as an idempotent skip instead of a mismatch warning; tests updated in functions/tests/test_bulk_callback_client.py and functions/tests/test_policy_rule_events.py.
[specifics: project: fleet-platform-app; branch: dev; error_message: Data collection rule has been modified before operation completed; test_file_ama_conflicts: functions/tests/test_ama_create_dcr_conflicts.py; test_file_bulk_callback: functions/tests/test_bulk_callback_client.py; test_file_policy_rule_events: functions/tests/test_policy_rule_events.py; removed_field: source_run_id excluded from governance registration payload; db_table: bulk_automation_runs; dcr_behavior: waits for canonical Rackspace DCR with expected rax-config-version and reuses it if present; janus_behavior: treat target-state-equals-current-state as idempotent skip instead of mismatch warning]", "2026-03-23: On 2026-03-23 for azr-fleet-platform PR #66, CI and review fixes were prepared locally before push. CI failure was caused by OpenAPI 3.1 Event Grid GET response schemas using nullable in api/openapi.yaml; fixed by converting those fields to type unions with 'null' so scripts/strip-v1-prefix.py generated a valid api/openapi-apim.yaml. Four live Copilot review comments were validated and fixed: (1) ama_assign_policies now distinguishes no_entitled_ama_policies from missing_entitled_ama_dcrs and returns missing_dcr_os_types; (2) select_policies_for_subscription no longer duplicates resolve_subscription_policy_assignments and derives policy keys from get_expected_artifacts_for_service_blocks; (3) http_eventgrid_subscription_get now validates customer/subscription ownership via customer_subscriptions before DB/Azure fallback and returns 404 on mismatch; (4) Azure fallback upsert now persists event_delivery_schema. Targeted tests passed: test_http_eventgrid.py, test_ama_targeting.py, test_misc_resource_change_baseline.py, and pre-commit passed. Local commits: 516f83a (Fix Event Grid OpenAPI null schemas), 8ea4b54 (Address PR 66 review findings).
[specifics: pr: azr-fleet-platform PR #66; commits: 516f83a (Fix Event Grid OpenAPI null schemas), 8ea4b54 (Address PR 66 review findings); openapi_issue: api/openapi.yaml used 'nullable' in OpenAPI 3.1 Event Grid GET response schemas; openapi_fix: converted nullable fields to type unions with 'null' so scripts/strip-v1-prefix.py produced valid api/openapi-apim.yaml; ama_assign_policies: distinguishes no_entitled_ama_policies from missing_entitled_ama_dcrs and returns missing_dcr_os_types; select_policies_for_subscription: removed duplication of resolve_subscription_policy_assignments; derives policy keys from get_expected_artifacts_for_service_blocks; http_eventgrid_subscription_get: validates customer/subscription ownership via customer_subscriptions before DB/Azure fallback and returns 404 on mismatch; azure_fallback_upsert: persists event_delivery_schema; tests_passed: test_http_eventgrid.py, test_ama_targeting.py, test_misc_resource_change_baseline.py; pre_commit: passed locally]", "2026-03-19: On 2026-03-19, PR #55 in fleet-platform-app had a failing Governance WAF GitHub Actions workflow caused by three workflow issues, not product code: (1) the manifest job hard-failed when AZR_COMPLIANCE_V3_REPOSITORY was unset, (2) cross-repo checkout of racker/azr-compliance-v3 failed without a dedicated AZR_COMPLIANCE_V3_READ_TOKEN secret, and (3) the workflow invoked python -m pytest without installing pytest. Fixed on branch dev with commits 48fa34b, 8747bd8, bd90e4f, and 0b173f9. Final workflow behavior: use racker/azr-compliance-v3 as default repo, skip external manifest verification with a warning when no read token is configured, and install pytest before running manifest unit tests.
[specifics: pr: fleet-platform-app#55; workflow: Governance WAF GitHub Actions; env_var_unset: AZR_COMPLIANCE_V3_REPOSITORY caused manifest job hard-fail; missing_secret: AZR_COMPLIANCE_V3_READ_TOKEN required for cross-repo checkout of racker/azr-compliance-v3; command_invoked: python -m pytest (pytest not installed); fix_branch: dev; fix_commits: 48fa34b, 8747bd8, bd90e4f, 0b173f9; default_repo: racker/azr-compliance-v3 set as default in workflow; workflow_behavior: skip external manifest verification with warning when no read token configured; install_pytest: pytest installed before running manifest unit tests]", "2026-03-23: On 2026-03-23 in azr-fleet-platform PR #67, five Copilot review findings were validated and fixed in commit 12fdec5. Fixes: AMA remediation start endpoint now generates a DB run_id up front and passes it into the orchestration so statusUrl/logsUrl use /v1/runs/{runId} instead of the Durable instance id; remediation timeout minutes are coerced/clamped to 1-240 via _coerce_remediation_timeout_minutes in both onboarding remediation loops; resolve_entitled_ama_os_types now returns a sorted list; http_ama 500 responses now return a stable safe message plus correlationId instead of raw exception text; explicit target_os_types in ama_setup_subscription now raise on invalid values instead of collapsing to [] and later expanding to both OSes. Targeted tests passed and all PR #67 review threads were replied to and resolved.
[specifics: repo_pr: azr-fleet-platform PR #67; commit: 12fdec5; copilot_review_findings_fixed: 5; remediation_start_endpoint: DB run_id generated up front; statusUrl/logsUrl=/v1/runs/{runId}; remediation_timeout_minutes: clamped to 1-240; _coerce_remediation_timeout_minutes: used in onboarding remediation loops; resolve_entitled_ama_os_types: now returns a sorted list; http_ama_500_response: returns stable safe message + correlationId (no raw exception text); ama_setup_subscription: explicit target_os_types invalid values now raise instead of collapsing to []]", "2026-03-19: On 2026-03-19 in fleet-platform-app, committed two related changes on branch dev. Commit 34a738f adds a governance WAF policy pipeline: listener-scoped governance WAF Bicep, route manifest generation/validation from azr-compliance-v3, smoke tests, and GitHub Actions workflow governance-waf.yml. Commit 8dc2f2e hardens the isolated webhook hot path against timeout storms in functions-webhook by adding in-memory token cache with background refresh, bounded DB and Service Bus timeouts, sender reuse, returning 503 on enqueue failure for retries, and raising host timeout to 5 minutes as a band-aid. Working tree was clean after both commits.
[specifics: commit_34a738f: adds governance WAF pipeline (listener-scoped Bicep, route manifest generation/validation, smoke tests, GitHub Actions workflow governance-waf.yml); host_timeout: 5 minutes; enqueue_failure_response: 503; workflow_file: governance-waf.yml; azr_compliance: azr-compliance-v3; waf_scope: listener-scoped governance WAF Bicep; function_app: functions-webhook; token_cache: in-memory with background refresh; bounded_timeouts: DB and Service Bus timeouts bounded]"]}
